summary: Problem 4 asks for a finite-free-probability analog of the Blachman-Stam Fisher information inequality, specifically the subharmonicity of 1/Phi_n under finite free convolution. While 40 of 45 issues are resolved (including critical Hessian dimensional errors and heat-flow commutativity), 5 open issues block a complete proof — most critically an unproven equivalence between the coefficient definition and the Walsh averaging formula, which undermines the foundational setup. The best proof attempt still reports 100 open issues and a low 16% solvability score, indicating it is far from complete.
problems: FOUNDATIONAL: Equivalence between the bilinear coefficient formula (c_k = sum_{i+j=k} ...) and the eq:walsh averaging formula is unproven — this is the definitional bedrock of the entire argument.

Reduction to simple roots is incomplete: simple roots of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) are not verified (Gap 3), leaving the limiting argument logically open despite a prior 'resolved' fix on the same theme.
LSC inversion in Step C does not handle the K=0 degenerate case (Gap 4), so the inequality inversion from liminf bounds is not valid in general.
Variance additivity (Prop. prop:props (i)) proof is incomplete, weakening a key structural property used downstream.
Best proof attempt (claude_opus_5-4) reports 100 open issues and only 16% solvability — current artifact is not close to a verified proof.
highlights: High resolution rate: 40 of 45 issues resolved, including hard ones like the JJ* vs J*J Hessian dimensional error, missing 1/n! Walsh normalization, and heat-flow commutativity.
Several factor-of-2 score-derivative concerns in Lemma 1 were investigated, numerically verified, and confirmed correct.
Lemma 2(iii) commutativity now has a self-contained proof replacing a one-line citation, improving rigor.

The core logical skeleton (heat-flow / score-function approach mirroring Blachman-Stam) appears sound per critic review, with gaps concentrated in setup equivalence and limiting arguments rather than the main mechanism.

suggested_todos.title: Prove equivalence of the coefficient formula and the eq:walsh averaging formula — this is the definitional prerequisite for all subsequent steps
Complete the simple-roots reduction by verifying simple roots of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) (Gap 3), ensuring consistency with the earlier 'resolved' limiting-argument fix
Handle the K=0 degenerate case in the Step C LSC inequality inversion (Gap 4)
Finish the variance additivity proof for Prop. prop:props (i), including the cross-term vanishing argument
Reconcile the 100-open-issue best-proof artifact with the 45-issue tracking list and re-run verification after the foundational equivalence is closed
